title: Proof Audit Persona Output
description: Strict structured output for a single persona (pedantic / adversarial / generous / codex-cross-model) in proof-audit Phase 2. Top-level is an object wrapping a `findings` array (OpenAI structured-output requires object root). Complex conditional validation (e.g. require latex_patch only when status != verified) is enforced by the host-side postflight script, not the schema.

claim_audit_count.description: Number of atomic claims actually inspected. Should match the size of the input claim list. Used by postflight to detect persona dropping claims silently.

finding.description: All fields are 'required' to satisfy OpenAI strict-mode structured output. For semantically optional fields, use empty string '' or empty array [] as the null value.

claim_id.description: Stable claim ID from Phase 1 (e.g., 'C3'). One finding per gap; same claim may produce multiple findings.

claim_text_excerpt.description: Verbatim 1-2 sentence quote from the claim being audited.

status.description: Verdict. Use 'verified' only when persona has actively checked AND found no issue; do not use it as a default placeholder.

gap_type.description: Required taxonomy. Use 'none' iff status == 'verified'. Use 'other' only if no enum value fits; if 'other', describe taxonomy in gap_description. The 52 substantive enums map 1-to-1 with references/common_proof_gaps.md.

confidence.description: Persona's subjective probability that this is a real gap (not its own misreading).

gap_description.description: 1-3 sentence explanation in plain English. Required even for verified claims (say which check was passed).

invoked_lemmas.description: External lemmas/theorems used at this step (e.g., 'Lemma A.1', 'Slutsky').

preconditions.description: Assumptions/regularity conditions the claim relies on.

latex_patch.description: Concrete LaTeX snippet that fixes the gap. Use empty string '' when status == 'verified'. When non-empty: (a) MUST start with `\hypertarget{<hypertarget_anchor>}{}%` on its own line — using the EXACT string from this finding's hypertarget_anchor field — so the patch site is traceable; (b) MUST reference real label names from the paper source (\ref/\eqref/\Cref to existing labels). Both rules are checked by lint_patches.py and a violation downgrades the finding to RESIDUAL.md.

insertion_hint.description: Where in the paper the patch should be inserted (e.g., 'Appendix A.2, before the DCT step'). Empty string when status == 'verified'.

hypertarget_anchor.description: Backward-traceable anchor. Format: 'gap_<claim_id>_<gap_type>' for findings, 'verified_<claim_id>' for verified status. Used as \hypertarget in patch.
